coaching.title: Easy Run — Close Out Peak Week
coaching.message: Easy aerobic run, ~9–12 miles, strictly Zone 2 (roughly 133–149 bpm, well under your 165 LTHR). This is the last session of the hardest week of the block — the job is to flush the legs, not add stress. If it feels hard, slow down or walk the hills. Keep it conversational start to finish. Peak fitness is banked; from here you only get fresher.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Top off with ~40g carbs before you head out. It's an easy effort so you won't need much on the move, but on anything over 75 min sip 30–40g carbs/hr to keep training your gut for the Alps. Hit the post-run window within 30 min: ~25–30g protein plus carbs to lock in the week's adaptations. Buenos Aires winter is dry — stay ahead on fluids and electrolytes.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: You just finished a heavy peak week, so treat today's easy run as recovery in motion. Legs up after: 10 min of light mobility, calves and hip flexors foam rolled, and prioritize sleep tonight — aim for 8+ hours. Adaptation from the whole week cashes in during the next 48 hours of rest.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: You've cleared the toughest week of the plan — sit with that. Julian Alps rewards patience: the discipline to run easy on easy days is the same discipline that keeps you steady when the climbs bite at 2,500m in September. Trust the taper ahead. The hay is in the barn.
